title: (Junior) Product Manager (m/w/d)
ai_benefits: Secure workplace in the Hannover region
Free lavera products worth 520 EUR per year
Givve card (tax-free benefit of 50 EUR/month after probation)
ai_core_responsibilities: Manage the development and optimization of the natural cosmetics product portfolio throughout the entire product lifecycle. Coordinate cross-functional projects with R&D, Marketing, and Supply Chain while analyzing market trends to provide strategic recommendations.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires a degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Product Management, or a similar field with initial to several years of experience in FMCG or cosmetics. Candidates should possess strong analytical skills, a solution-oriented mindset, and the ability to manage multiple parallel projects.
description_text:
Auf der Suche nach Naturtalenten!
Von der Produktidee bis zur Auslieferung erfolgt alles aus einer Hand. Seit über 30 Jahren stellen wir lavera („die Wahre“) Naturkosmetik her. So facettenreich wie die lavera Produktwelt sind auch die vielen Aufgabenbereiche bei der Laverana GmbH & Co. KG. Du suchst Abwechslung, ein super Team und tägliche Herausforderungen? Dann suchen wir dich für unser laverana-Team!

Bitte beachte, dass die Position(-en) aktuell bis Ende 2027 befristet sind. Eine weitere Zusammenarbeit wird aber angestrebt!
Diese spannenden Aufgaben warten auf dich
In dieser Position wirkst du an der Entwicklung, Steuerung und Weiterentwicklung unseres Produktportfolios im Bereich Naturkosmetik mit.
Die Planung, Koordination und Begleitung von Produktneuheiten und Produktoptimierungen entlang des gesamten Produktlebenszyklus liegen in deinem Aufgabenbereich.
Gemeinsam mit internen Schnittstellen wie Forschung & Entwicklung, Marketing, Einkauf, Vertrieb und Supply Chain treibst du Produktprojekte strukturiert voran.
Markt-, Wettbewerbs- und Verbrauchertrends analysierst du fortlaufend und leitest daraus relevante Handlungsempfehlungen für das Produktmanagement ab.
Entscheidungsvorlagen, Präsentationen und produktbezogene Unterlagen bereitest du adressatengerecht und sorgfältig auf.
Auch die Abstimmung von Timings, Projektfortschritten und bereichsübergreifenden Anforderungen gehört zu deinem Verantwortungsbereich.
Die Einführung neuer Produkte begleitest du aktiv und unterstützt dabei, eine erfolgreiche Positionierung im Markt sicherzustellen.
Darüber hinaus pflegst und aktualisierst du produktbezogene Daten und trägst zu einer verlässlichen Datenbasis im Produktmanagement bei.
Dein Profil: Damit begeisterst du uns
Ein abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Betriebswirtschaft, Marketing, Produktmanagement oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation bringst du mit.
Erste bis mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Produktmanagement, idealerweise im FMCG-, Kosmetik- oder Markenartikelumfeld, konntest du bereits sammeln.
Ein gutes Gespür für Marktanforderungen, Produkte und Verbraucherbedürfnisse zeichnet dich aus.
Strukturiertes, eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten sowie ein sicherer Umgang mit mehreren parallelen Themen sind für dich selbstverständlich.
Kommunikationsstärke, Organisationsvermögen und Teamfähigkeit gehören zu deinen Stärken.
Eine analytische Denkweise sowie eine sorgfältige und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise runden dein Profil ab.
Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Fachbereichen und daran, Themen aktiv voranzutreiben, bringst du ebenfalls mit.
Neben guten Englischkenntnissen wäre eine weitere Sprache wünschenswert.
Benefits: Das bieten wir dir
Wir lieben und leben ein offenes Miteinander
Einen sicheren Arbeitsplatz in der Region Hannover mit guten Verkehrsanbindungen
Die Möglichkeit an 1 Tag/Woche im HomeOffice zu arbeiten
30 Urlaubstage im Jahr
Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld
Kostenlose lavera-Produkte im Wert von 520 EUR im Jahr
Gehalts-Extra - givve® Card (steuerfreier Sachbezug in Höhe von 50,- €/Monat nach Ablauf der Probezeit)
Zuschuss zur Kinderbetreuung (bis zu 100,- €/Monat) sowie Anspruch auf 2 Wochen bezahlten Vaterschaftsurlaub nach der Geburt
Hansefit-Mitgliedschaft
Zuschuss zum Jobticket
Weiterbildungs- und Fortbildungsmöglichkeiten
Mitarbeitendenrabatt in unserem Onlineshop
Corporate Benefits
Kostenlose Getränke und Obst
Regelmäßige Events für unsere Mitarbeitenden (Sommerfest, Weihnachtsfeier, Teamevents, Gesundheitswochen, Firmenläufe, etc.)
Bitte beachte, dass unsere Angebote variieren können! 
Das ist dein Job? Dann bewirb dich!
Wir haben dein Interesse geweckt? Du möchtest Teil des laverana-Teams werden? Sende uns deine Bewerbungsunterlagen über unser Bewerberportal, indem du auf „Jetzt bewerben“ klickst. Bei Fragen zur Stelle oder zum Prozess, wende dich gerne an Matthias Steege unter der Telefonnummer 0511 67541 226.

title: Product Manager IV
ai_core_responsibilities: Drive product outcomes for a kidney care platform by defining vision, strategy, and OKRs. Lead the full product lifecycle from discovery through delivery, collaborating with engineering, design, and stakeholders to solve complex business problems.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires 4+ years of product management experience in an Agile environment with strong analytical and communication skills. Preference is given to candidates with experience in healthcare technology and 0-to-1 product development.
description_text:
Company Description
Technology is our how. And people are our why. For over two decades, we have been harnessing technology to drive meaningful change.

By combining world-class engineering, industry expertise and a people-centric mindset, we consult and partner with leading brands from various industries to create dynamic platforms and intelligent digital experiences that drive innovation and transform businesses.

From prototype to real-world impact - be part of a global shift by doing work that matters.
Job Description
*Remote in USA- Prefer local candidates to Denver, CO (If local may require 3 days on site per month)
Our digital product team is critical to enabling our kidney care platform, and our product team is growing! We have an opportunity for a Product Manager to drive product outcomes which solve critical business problems. Your primary activities will include defining vision, strategy, and OKRs for your product area, and then executing your product vision across one or many applications for one or many user groups.
We are looking for someone who is an expert relationship builder, has a bias towards action, can collaborate with cross-functional teams, and is obsessive about breaking down complex problems into product solutions with their engineering, design, and stakeholder teams.
Qualifications
Product Execution & Delivery
Lead product initiatives through the full product lifecycle, from discovery through delivery.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, end users, designers, and engineering teams to ensure solutions align with business goals and user needs.
Facilitate story mapping and backlog refinement activities to translate product requirements into actionable development work.
Identify and manage cross-functional dependencies that impact product delivery.
Prepare for and actively participate in roadmap planning, sprint planning, and agile ceremonies.
Partner with Legal, Compliance, Security, and Risk teams to ensure products meet all regulatory and organizational requirements.
Support the development of user communications, training materials, and educational resources related to product features and capabilities.
Product Discovery & Strategy
Partner with Product Leadership to define and refine the vision, strategy, and roadmap for your product area.
Collaborate with Engineering, UX, and Research teams to conduct discovery activities that uncover user needs, pain points, and opportunities.
Work closely with UX teams to design intuitive user experiences and validate solutions through rapid feedback cycles and usability testing.
Conduct user interviews, field observations, and stakeholder engagement sessions to gain a deep understanding of customer challenges and workflows.
Collaborate with clinical, operational, provider, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities that improve user experiences and business outcomes.
Develop a strong understanding of organizational priorities and business challenges to ensure product investments deliver measurable value.
Required Qualifications
4+ years of product management experience in an Agile environment, with a focus on digital products.
Strong business acumen, intellectual curiosity, and a passion for driving outcomes.
Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, organizational, and communication skills.
Ability to clearly articulate product vision and inspire teams around the "why" behind solutions.
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities, make informed trade-offs, and deliver results in fast-paced environments.
Adaptability and resilience in organizations experiencing rapid change and growth.
Demonstrated ability to quickly build expertise in new domains and industries.
Strong relationship-building skills with experience collaborating across technical, clinical, operational, and executive stakeholder groups.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience building healthcare technology products and leading 0-to-1 product development initiatives from concept through launch.
Experience writing product requirements, user stories, and feature specifications.
Strong proficiency in data analysis, spreadsheet modeling, presentations, and business case development.
Experience supporting the delivery of web and mobile applications.
Prior experience working within healthcare organizations or regulated industries.
Additional Information
Discover some of the global benefits that empower our people to become the best version of themselves:
Finance: Competitive salary package, share plan, company performance bonuses, value-based recognition awards, referral bonus; 
Career Development: Career coaching, global career opportunities, non-linear career paths, internal development programmes for management and technical leadership;
Learning Opportunities: Complex projects, rotations, internal tech communities, training, certifications, coaching, online learning platforms subscriptions, pass-it-on sessions, workshops, conferences;
Work-Life Balance: Hybrid work and flexible working hours, employee assistance programme;
Health: Global internal wellbeing programme, access to wellbeing apps;
Community: Global internal tech communities, hobby clubs and interest groups, inclusion and diversity programmes, events and celebrations.
Additional Employee Requirements 
Participation in both internal meetings and external meetings via video calls, as necessary. 
Ability to go into corporate or client offices to work onsite, as necessary. 
Prolonged periods of remaining stationary at a desk and working on a computer, as necessary. 
Ability to bend, kneel, crouch, and reach overhead, as necessary. 
Hand-eye coordination necessary to operate computers and various pieces of office equipment, as necessary. 
Vision abilities including close vision, toleration of fluorescent lighting, and adjusting focus, as necessary. 
For positions that require business travel and/or event attendance, ability to lift 25 lbs, as necessary. 
For positions that require business travel and/or event attendance, a valid driver’s license and acceptable driving record are required, as driving is an essential job function.
*If requested, reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees requiring accommodations to perform the essential functions of their jobs, absent undue hardship. 
USA Benefits (Full time roles only, does not apply to contractor positions)
Robust healthcare and benefits including Medical, Dental, vision, Disability coverage, and various other benefit options 
Flexible Spending Accounts (Medical, Transit, and Dependent Care) 
Employer Paid Life Insurance and AD&D Coverages 
Health Savings account paired with our low-cost High Deductible Medical Plan 
401(k) Safe Harbor Retirement plan with employer match with immediately vest 
At Endava, we’re committed to creating an open, inclusive, and respectful environment where everyone feels safe, valued, and empowered to be their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives—because we know that inclusive teams help us deliver smarter, more innovative solutions for our customers. Hiring decisions are based on merit, skills, qualifications, and potential. If you need adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Product Manager
ai_core_responsibilities: The Product Manager will own the end-to-end mobile product roadmap, focusing on discovery, experimentation, and launch. They will collaborate with founders, design, engineering, and operations to build features that drive engagement and retention.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates need 3+ years of product management experience, specifically with mobile-first products and engineering teams. Strong analytical skills and an entrepreneurial mindset are required to translate insights into execution plans.
description_text:
About the Role
As Product Manager, Mobile, you'll work directly with founders, design, engineering, and operations to build intuitive, high-impact features that improve engagement, retention, and user outcomes. You'll own the mobile product roadmap end-to-end - from discovery and experimentation through to launch and iteration.
What We're Looking For
3+ years of experience in Product Management, preferably building mobile-first products
Experience working closely with mobile engineering teams (Flutter, iOS, or Android)
Strong analytical skills and comfort working with product and business metrics
Ability to translate customer insights into clear product requirements and execution plans
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Entrepreneurial mindset with a bias for action and ownership

title: Senior Product Manager, Banco (Consumer App)
ai_core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end Banco consumer experience, including onboarding, multi-currency wallets, and card issuing across web and mobile. Focus on increasing user activation and retention while ensuring high reliability and localization for a global user base.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires extensive experience owning B2C or consumer-grade fintech apps with a strong instinct for mobile-first UX and payment systems. Candidates must be fluent in product metrics and have practical experience using AI in their daily product workflow.
description_text:
Papaya Global is a rapidly growing, award-winning B2B tech unicorn with an ambitious mission to revolutionize the payroll & payments industry. With over $400M raised from multiple tier-one investors, our innovative technology provides a comprehensive solution for managing global workforces, encompassing everything from hiring and onboarding to managing and paying employees in over 160 countries.
We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own the Banco consumer experience end to end. This is a rare seat: a genuinely consumer-grade fintech product — wallet, card issuing, KYC/onboarding, withdrawals — that reaches a global, mass user base, but distributed through enterprise customers rather than paid acquisition. You will own the roadmap, priorities, and execution for the app workers touch every time they get paid, and you will be accountable for making that experience fast, reliable, and effortless at scale.
You will report to the Director of Product leading the Payments applicative group and work in a squad model — a dedicated design, analytics, and engineering team — with a mandate to raise the quality bar of the app while growing activation and retention among the workers our customers onboard.
What you’ll own
The end-to-end Banco consumer experience: onboarding and identity verification (KYC), the multi-currency wallet, card issuing (virtual and physical), and withdrawals to bank and stablecoin — across web and mobile.
Activation and retention: turning invited workers into active, paid, engaged users; reducing drop-off across onboarding and first-payout funnels.
Reliability and quality: driving down errors, support tickets, and friction so that “I got paid” is boring and dependable.
Localization at scale: making the experience feel native across 160+ countries and dozens of payout rails, banking conventions, and languages.
The consumer data model: real objects (addresses, bank accounts, identity) that power smart UX — IBAN→bank lookup, zip→address autofill, one-time data entry instead of repeated forms.

Requirements
Consumer product experience. You have owned a B2C or consumer-grade app — ideally in fintech, neobanking, payments, wallets, or gig-economy payouts — where the user is an individual, not a business, and the bar is “make it effortless.” This is the core requirement for this role.
Mobile-first UX instinct. You think in funnels, activation, and retention, and you have measurably improved onboarding or engagement in a consumer app.
Payments or fintech depth. You understand money movement — wallets, cards, KYC/AML, payout rails — or you have shown you can master a complex, regulated domain fast.
Metrics fluency. You quote baseline → intervention → result numbers, know how each metric was measured, and know its weaknesses.
Correctness instinct for money. You grasp that in money movement a wrong payout costs far more than a blocked one, and you design for that asymmetry.
Individual ownership. You can tell at least one product story entirely in “I” terms — the decision you personally made, the alternative you rejected, and what happened.
Practical AI fluency (2026 bar). You use AI daily in your own product work and can describe last week’s usage concretely; bonus if you have shipped AI features and understand evals, failure modes, and human-in-the-loop.

Nice to have
Cross-border / multi-currency payments, card issuing, or KYC-provider integration experience.
Experience taking a consumer app from early/unstable to reliable at scale.
Experience in both a large PM org and a scrappy, 0→1 environment.
Hands-on building — you ship small things without waiting for engineers.

title: Area Product Manager – Entry-Level Analytical Instruments
ai_core_responsibilities: The Area Product Manager is responsible for identifying and acquiring new customers and managing long-term relationships for analytical instruments in Belgium and Luxembourg. This includes planning sales activities, executing campaigns, and providing technical advice on density, viscosity, and refractive index measurements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a scientific background in Chemistry, Applied Physics, or a related field and be fluent in Dutch, French, and English. A valid Category B driver's license and a proactive, entrepreneurial approach to sales are required.
description_text:
Company Description
We are looking for a motivated Area Product Manager to strengthen our sales team.
In this role, you will be responsible for selling our entry-level, high-quality analytical instruments for measuring density, viscosity, and refractive index across Belgium and Luxembourg.
Are you ready to combine your chemistry background with your commercial experience in an international and innovative company?
Apply today and become part of our team!
Job Description
Proactively identify, approach, and acquire new customers, from initial contact through to contract negotiation and closing.
Support customers throughout the purchasing process by providing tailored advice on analytical instrumentation.
Independently plan and organize customer visits, appointments, and sales activities.
Develop and execute sales campaigns across a variety of channels.
Represent our products at trade fairs, exhibitions, and conferences.
Build and maintain long-term relationships with existing customers.
Advise customers on technical applications and develop customer-specific solutions.
Qualifications
You have a scientific background, preferably in Chemistry, Applied Physics, or a related field.
Experience in the sales or marketing of process instrumentation is a strong advantage.
You are fluent in both Dutch and French, with professional proficiency in English.
You possess excellent communication and organizational skills.
You are customer-focused, persuasive, and entrepreneurial, with a proactive and independent approach to work.
You hold a valid Category B driver's license and are willing to travel regularly throughout Belgium and Luxembourg.
Additional Information
A varied and challenging role in a pleasant working environment where teamwork and collaboration are at the heart of everything we do.
A comprehensive onboarding and training program, including personalized training and development.
Ongoing support for your professional growth within a dynamic and collaborative team.
A competitive compensation package, including a company car, laptop, and smartphone.
An attractive benefits package, including additional fringe benefits and generous vacation entitlement.

title: Product Manager
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship high-quality digital products that align with business goals and user needs. Integrate AI capabilities into product strategies and manage the end-to-end delivery process from refinement to shipping.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires 3-5 years of experience in digital product management with a strong understanding of AI applications and agile methodologies. Must possess excellent communication skills and the ability to manage client relationships and stakeholders effectively.
description_text:
About us
We're an independent European digital product studio with a strong track record of delivering market-defining strategies and products for enterprises and governments. With about 200 talented people across three countries, premium AI capabilities and a diverse portfolio of international clients, we define, design and ship high-quality digital products, strategies and software. From global cloud-based platforms and large-scale apps to medical devices, banking software and augmented reality experiences. In a nutshell: if it lives and breathes digital, it’s what we do best.
Become a part of our team
At In The Pocket, you join a multidisciplinary team of designers, engineers, strategists and data specialists who care about the work as much as the people they work with. We work in small, autonomous teams that stay close to our clients and their users, and we give everyone the space to take ownership, speak up and grow. You’ll learn from colleagues across our studios, ship products that reach thousands or even millions of people and help shape what digital can do for leading organisations in Europe.
What you’ll do 
As a Product Manager at In The Pocket, you will work in a cross-functional product team with a crystal-clear mission: create digital products that make people happy and businesses grow.
Product thinking
You are developing the tools & reflexes that allow you to build products, independently from technology or platform. You can identify platform-specific opportunities and risks and understand how they might impact the product strategy.
You understand the fundamentals of building great products, both from a design and a technical perspective.
You have a strong interest in AI and understand how AI technologies can enhance products and create value. You're able to identify opportunities where AI can improve user experience, automate processes, or unlock new business models. You stay current with AI trends and can translate their potential into actionable product initiatives.
Product Strategy & Definition
You work closely with the team to understand, refine and articulate the product challenge(s) we're trying to solve. You facilitate the exploration of multiple solutions to these challenges and help the team choose the best trade-off between business, user and technology. You understand the client's long-term business strategy and can incorporate this.
You prioritize the ideas into a roadmap together with the client and your team.
You ensure the product vision and strategy are understood and shared by your entire team and all stakeholders.
You explore how AI and emerging technologies can be integrated into the product strategy to deliver innovative solutions.
Product Delivery
You can facilitate the entire refinement process of a low- to medium complexity product (e.g. a one-platform product, limited external dependencies, smaller to medium-sized team, mature or emerging technology). You ensure product requirements are clear and ready to implement in a timely manner.
You ensure there is a continued & shared understanding of priorities and trade-offs. You take ownership of ensuring we don't just shape quality products, but also ship them.
Insight-driven
You identify, track and analyze key product success metrics.
You conduct user research activities and report findings to a diverse audience in a clear and qualitative way.
You use data and user insights to validate assumptions, especially when exploring AI-driven features or new product directions.
People & Collaboration
You are a natural collaborator and team player. You build strong relationships with designers, developers, and stakeholders, creating an environment of trust and open communication.
You excel at bringing people together around a shared vision. You listen actively, show empathy, and know how to motivate your team to do their best work.
You handle conflict constructively and can navigate different opinions to find solutions that work for everyone.
You're comfortable giving and receiving feedback, and you help create a culture of continuous improvement within your team.
Who you are 
You have 3-5 years of experience in Product Management, working on digital products (mobile, web, SaaS, or AI-powered applications).
You have essential knowledge of AI and its applications in digital products. You understand how tools like ChatGPT, machine learning models, intelligent automation, or AI-powered features can transform user experiences. You're excited to explore and integrate AI capabilities into your products.
You have mastered core Product Management fundamentals and have solid experience working within agile development processes. You're a quick learner who is eager to continue expanding your skill set and knowledge.
You have a solid understanding of human-centered design and best-practices in digital customer experience.
You have strong organizational skills and can handle multiple projects and tasks efficiently.
You have excellent people skills and emotional intelligence. You're able to read the room, understand different perspectives, and bring stakeholders together. You communicate with clarity, empathy, and confidence.
You can navigate and align multiple stakeholders with different needs and priorities.
You are confident and comfortable working in a client-facing role. You build genuine, trusted relationships with clients.
What we offer 
Joining In The Pocket means becoming part of a team that shapes the future of our clients’ businesses across Europe. Here’s what you can look forward to:
You’ll start with a permanent contract in a role that comes with responsibility and the opportunity to make a real impact from day one.
We offer a competitive salary that reflects your experience and the value you bring.
Our benefits package has you covered with hospitalisation and group insurance, meal vouchers, a phone subscription, and more.
You’ll have a mobility budget that lets you decide how to get around, your way. 
Life at In The Pocket isn’t just about work, we host team events throughout the year to celebrate, connect, and have fun together.
Learning never stops here, so after six months, you’ll receive a dedicated learning and development budget to keep growing your skills and exploring new horizons.

title: AI Product Manager
ai_core_responsibilities: Define the product strategy and roadmap for AI capabilities and scientific AI assistants within the Code Ocean platform. Rapidly prototype new AI experiences and translate successful experiments into scalable, production-ready product capabilities.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires a technical background in Computer Science with at least three years of experience in product ownership and hands-on development of AI-native products. Must be proficient in prototyping with LLMs and agents, with a strong focus on reproducibility and compliance in scientific research.
description_text:
Science is about to change more in the next decade than it has during the entire last century. AI agents will run experiments, test hypotheses, and explore solution spaces at a scale no human team ever could - and computational science will be where it happens first.
Code Ocean is building the platform where that future becomes real: the AI-native environment where teams of scientists and fleets of AI agents work side by side on the world's most important computational problems, from early drug discovery to translational research. The world's leading pharmaceutical companies, biotechs, and research organizations already run their science on Code Ocean — because we pair that ambition with the reproducibility, compliance, and security that real science demands.

About the Role
We’re looking for an AI Product Manager to shape the future of scientific AI. You’ll own the evolution of our scientific AI assistant, helping researchers perform complex computational work faster, more reliably, and in a way that organizations can confidently deploy at enterprise scale.
This role sits at the seam between Product and R&D and is designed for someone who has lived on both sides: an engineer who became a product manager, or a product manager who still enjoys building.
This position includes spending time with customers and understanding emerging workflows, rapidly prototyping new experiences using AI coding agents, defining evaluation frameworks for agentic systems, and translating successful experiments into scalable product capabilities.
You’ll help determine not only what we build, but how AI becomes a trusted collaborator for computational scientists.


What you’ll do
Define product strategy and roadmap for key AI capabilities across the Code Ocean platform.
Work directly with computational scientists and AI teams to uncover opportunities where GenAI can meaningfully improve scientific workflows.
Rapidly prototype new product experiences using AI coding assistants and validate them with users before engineering implementation.
Transform successful customer experiments and proofs-of-concept into scalable product capabilities.
Write clear product specifications and user stories that align customer needs with the platform’s technical architecture.
Define evaluation frameworks, benchmarks, and success metrics for agentic systems, balancing scientific accuracy, reproducibility, usability, and performance.
Partner closely with engineering, design, and scientific stakeholders to deliver high-impact AI features.

Drive adoption through continuous experimentation, product analytics, and customer feedback.


Requirements
You’re a software engineer at heart with substantial hands-on development experience and enjoy working close to the code.
You have built and shipped AI-native products powered by LLMs, agents, or modern AI services.
You are comfortable prototyping ideas yourself using AI coding assistants and understand how to turn prototypes into production-ready products.
You have experience translating complex AI capabilities into detailed product specifications and engineering requirements.
You actively follow advances in GenAI, agentic systems, evaluation methodologies, and AI developer tooling, and enjoy experimenting with emerging technologies.
You have at least three years of experience owning products, including strategy, prioritization, roadmaps, KPIs, and cross-functional execution.
You enjoy partnering with customers to understand difficult problems and validating solutions through rapid iteration.
You’re excited by the challenge of building trustworthy AI for scientific research, where reproducibility, compliance, and explainability are first-class product requirements.
You hold a Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science or a related technical field.
Experience in building AI products for regulated industries, life sciences, healthcare, or research environments is a strong plus.

Why This Role
Large language models are changing how science is done—but scientific AI demands more than a chat interface that produces impressive-looking figures and explanations. It requires systems that researchers can trust, organizations can govern, and enterprises can deploy responsibly across many and large teams.
You’ll help define how AI agents become reliable collaborators in computational research while working with the world’s leading pharmaceutical companies and research organizations. If you’re excited about building AI products that solve technically demanding, high-impact problems, we’d love to talk. 



Equal Employment Opportunity
Code Ocean is committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law

title: Group Product Manager – Product Growth

address.addressLocality: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel

ai_core_responsibilities: Lead a team of Product Managers to develop and scale high-impact enterprise AI solutions from concept to production. Own a portfolio of solutions for Fortune 500 customers, balancing rapid execution with long-term scalability and productization.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires over 7 years of product management experience, including at least 2 years of experience leading other PMs in high-growth environments. Must demonstrate a proven track record of navigating ambiguity to deliver complex systems for enterprise customers.
description_text:
About Unframe
Unframe is an AI-first startup helping the world’s largest enterprises bring LLM-powered applications to life in days - not months. We combine the speed of a product company with the flexibility of a consultancy, helping customers move from idea to deployed AI systems faster than anyone else in the market.
Backed by Bessemer, Craft, and TLV Partners with $100M in Series B funding, we’re building a fast-growing, revenue-generating company working with Fortune 500 customers globally
About the role
We’re looking for a Group Product Manager (GPM) to lead a team of Product Managers and own a portfolio of enterprise AI solutions in production.
This is a highly ambiguous, fast-paced environment where many problems are not predefined. You will be expected to deeply understand customer needs, identify the right problems to solve, and lead your team in building solutions that deliver meaningful business value.
You will operate as a product leader with end-to-end ownership, shaping opportunities, driving execution, and scaling successful solutions.
What you’ll do
Lead a team of Product Managers working on complex, high-impact AI solutions in production

Work closely with customers and internal teams to identify high-value problems and define what to build

Operate in high ambiguity, shaping opportunities where requirements are not clearly defined

Drive the development of new solutions from early stages to production, making key product and trade-off decisions

Own a portfolio of solutions across one or more enterprise customers, setting priorities across multiple parallel initiatives

Balance speed of execution with long-term scalability, quality, and product thinking

Identify patterns across solutions and push for reuse and productization

Mentor and develop Product Managers, helping them operate effectively in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment
What we’re looking for
7+ years of product management experience, including 2+ years leading Product Managers, in high-growth or complex environments

Experience managing strong PMs, setting direction while empowering ownership and independent decision-making

Ability to operate in ambiguity, define problems, and lead teams in building new solutions from early stages to production

Proven track record of driving products or systems from concept to real-world impact, balancing speed, quality, and scalability

Experience working on complex systems and with enterprise customers, navigating competing needs and constraints

Strong cross-functional leadership and communication skills, working closely with engineering, AI, GTM, and customers
Why this role is unique
This isn’t a typical product role. You won’t inherit a roadmap, you’ll help define what’s worth building, working on real problems with real enterprise customers. You’ll operate in a true 0→1 environment at scale, taking ideas all the way to production. From day one, you’ll have real ownership, shaping direction, making decisions, and leading a team through ambiguity. You’ll work closely with founders and senior leaders in a fast-moving company where things actually get built.

title: AI Product Manager

address.addressLocality: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel

ai_core_responsibilities: The AI Product Manager will translate customer problems into structured solution plans and own the end-to-end delivery of AI-powered projects. They will collaborate with engineering and AI specialists to define prompt structures and surface reusable product patterns.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates need 4+ years of experience in product management or technical delivery with a strong background in working with engineering teams. Excellent written and verbal English communication skills and the ability to manage complex projects in fast-paced environments are required.
description_text:
About Us
Unframe is an AI-first startup helping the world’s largest enterprises bring LLM-powered applications to life in days - not months. We combine the speed of a product company with the flexibility of a consultancy, helping customers move from idea to deployed AI systems faster than anyone else in the market.
Backed by Bessemer, Craft, and TLV Partners with $100M in Series B funding, we’re building a fast-growing, revenue-generating company working with Fortune 500 customers globally
About the Role
As an AI Product Manager on our team, you’ll work directly with customers and internal teams to define, scope, and deliver AI-powered solutions using Unframe’s platform. You’ll bridge product thinking with real-world use cases, enabling fast, reliable delivery of custom solutions built on a shared core. We work on-site from our Tel Aviv office, collaborating closely across engineering, product, and AI to move fast and build with purpose.
This is an opportunity to work with top-tier global customers and solve real, high-impact problems.
What you’ll do
Translate customer problems into structured solution plans using Unframe.ai’s platform

Own the delivery of end-to-end projects in collaboration with engineers and AI specialists.

Write detailed specs and define prompt/context structures for solution logic.

Prioritize requirements and manage tradeoffs across speed, stability, and scope.

Work closely with the Platform team to surface reusable product patterns.

Serve as the voice of the user — from enterprise operators to domain experts.
What we are looking for
4+ years of experience in product management or technical delivery roles.

Experience working closely with engineering teams and technical products.

Ability to manage complex projects with multiple moving parts.

Strong written communication, systems thinking, and attention to detail.

Fluent English speaker - written and verbal communication must be excellent.

Comfortable working in fast-paced environments with evolving requirements.
Why Join Us
At Unframe, you’ll work on real AI products used by real customers, not experiments or demos. This role offers a unique opportunity to sit at the intersection of customer problems, product strategy, and AI execution, with true ownership over solutions from idea to delivery. You’ll collaborate with strong engineers and AI specialists, influence how our platform evolves, and help shape reusable product capabilities as we scale. If you’re motivated by impact, complexity, and building practical AI systems that actually ship, you’ll find this role both challenging and rewarding.

title: Product Manager
ai_core_responsibilities: The Product Manager oversees the strategic growth and full lifecycle of industrial eyewear and protective products from concept to commercialization. This includes leading new product development, managing supplier relationships, and collaborating with sales and marketing to drive profitable growth.
ai_requirements_summary: Requires a bachelor's degree in business, operations, or engineering and at least 3 years of product management experience, preferably in PPE. Candidates should possess strong analytical skills and familiarity with industrial safety standards like ANSI Z87.1.
description_text:
The Product Manager is responsible for the strategic management and growth of
the industrial eyewear and protective products portfolio. This role oversees the
product lifecycle from concept through commercialization, leveraging market
insights, customer feedback, and competitive analysis to guide product strategy
and business decisions. Working closely with cross-functional teams, the Product
Manager drives innovation, portfolio optimization, and profitable growth while
ensuring alignment with organizational objectives. The position requires strong
product management expertise, with experience in personal protective equipment
(PPE) and/or industrial eyewear serving as a valuable asset.

 

Job Responsibilities

 * Product Strategy & Development: Identify market opportunities and define
   strategic plans for assigned product categories, incorporating customer
   insights, competitive analysis, and industry trends to maximize profitable
   growth.
 * New Product Development: Lead new product development efforts from concept
   through commercialization, partnering with Suppliers and Engineering to
   translate market needs into innovative, manufacturable eyewear and PPE
   solutions.
 * Lifecycle Management: Own the full product lifecycle from concept to
   end-of-life, ensuring timely execution of product development, sourcing, and
   launch initiatives.
 * Sales & Marketing Collaboration: Partner with Sales and Marketing to develop
   go-to-market strategies, promotional campaigns, and product messaging.
 * Supplier & Inventory Management: Collaborate with global sourcing teams and
   suppliers to ensure product quality, cost-effectiveness, and on-time
   availability. Manage excess and obsolete inventory plans.
 * Quality & Compliance: Coordinate with testing agencies on compliance and
   standards testing (e.g., ANSI Z87.1 and related industrial eyewear
   standards). Monitor and resolve customer complaints and quality issues.
 * Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyze sales performance, inventory levels, and
   market data to guide forecasting and decision-making.
 * Cross-functional Leadership: Serve as the product advocate, aligning Sales,
   Engineering, Production, and Operations around shared product initiatives and
   business goals.
 * Customer & Market Engagement: Partner with Marketing and Sales on Voice of
   Customer (VoC) research, attend industry trade shows, and support field sales
   activities to gather market feedback and drive product adoption.
 * Travel: Up to 25% travel within North America for field sales support,
   distributor meetings, and industry events. Occasional international travel to
   suppliers and sourcing partners may be required.

 

Core Skills & Competency Requirements

 * Bachelor’s degree in business, Operations, Engineering, or a related field.
 * 3+ years of experience in Product Management, preferably within an
   industrial, safety, or PPE-related business.
 * Familiarity with industrial eyewear standards (e.g., ANSI Z87.1) or
   comparable PPE compliance frameworks is a plus.
 * Proven ability to apply financial principles, market dynamics, and business
   strategy to drive product profitability and sustainable growth in an
   industrial setting.
 * Collaborative mindset with a track record of partnering across Sales,
   Marketing, Finance, and Operations to deliver solutions.
 * Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data and translate it
   into strategic decisions.
 * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to engage
   stakeholders at all levels.
 * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook);
   experience with PLM or ERP systems (e.g., SAP) a plus.
 * Comfortable multitasking, working independently, and thriving in a fast-paced
   environment.
